subject: English Language Arts / Reading Support
grade_subject_fit: Post-reading Grade 8/9 literacy intervention unit for Long Way Down.
unit_context: Students have finished reading Long Way Down and now return to key moments to analyze character impact, symbolism, author or artist choice, theme, and Will's final decision.
assignment_purpose: Students build an Elevator Evidence Board using drawing, acting, mapping, speaking, and writing as entry points into evidence-based analysis.
final_evidence_target: Elevator Evidence Board plus a short oral or written defence answering: What does Will decide at the end, and what evidence proves your thinking?
student_task_flow: Analyze one author or artist choice.
Build and test a theme statement.
Rehearse the claim through discussion or freeze-frame.
Complete an oral or written defence.
success_criteria: I can make a clear claim about Will's decision.
I can explain how two ghosts affect Will.
I can explain one symbol and connect it to theme.
I can explain one author or artist choice.
I can explain my evidence clearly.

supports_scaffolds.label: Entry points
supports_scaffolds.support: Students may begin by drawing, acting, mapping, speaking, or writing.

supports_scaffolds.label: Sentence frames
supports_scaffolds.support: Students use repeated evidence frames to explain their thinking.

supports_scaffolds.label: Conference option
supports_scaffolds.support: Students may complete the defence orally with the teacher.

differentiation_model.support_pathway: Use pre-selected ghost cards, boxed board template, drawing labels, oral rehearsal, and sentence frames.
differentiation_model.core_pathway: Complete organizer pages and build a complete evidence board with a written or oral defence.
differentiation_model.extension_pathway: Compare possible endings, add a nonfiction connection, or compare verse and graphic novel choices.

checkpoint_release_logic: Two ghost entries and one symbol entry before board building.
teacher_implementation_notes: Do not reread the full book.
Keep analysis anchored in Will and the text.
Freeze-frame acting must be silent and symbolic.
No weapon gestures or improvised violence scenes.
likely_misconceptions: A drawing is enough without explanation.
A symbol can be named without being connected to theme.
standards: BC ELA 8/9 - oral, written, visual, and digital texts
BC ELA 8/9 - text features and literary devices
BC ELA 8/9 - transform ideas and information

phases.label: Day 1 Elevator Map
phases.teacher_move: Rebuild elevator sequence with floor cards.
phases.student_move: Map ghosts and one pressure word each.

phases.label: Day 2 Ghost Impact
phases.student_move: Analyze two ghosts using chosen entry point.

phases.label: Day 3 Symbol
phases.student_move: Sketch, label, and explain one symbol.

phases.label: Day 5 Theme
phases.teacher_move: Sort topic cards and build theme statements.

pacing_guide.buffer_note: If behind, reduce gallery feedback and preserve board build and defence.

teacher_guide.big_idea: Ambiguous endings require readers to build an argument from evidence.
teacher_guide.project_prompt: Create an Elevator Evidence Board that answers: What does Will decide at the end, and what evidence proves your thinking?
learning_goals: Explain one author or artist choice.
Present thinking visually, orally, or in writing.
teacher_notes: Choice is an entry point, not a replacement for explanation.
Teacher conference may replace written paragraph for support-pathway students.

task_sheet.title: Student Learning Guide - Elevator Evidence Project
task_sheet.purpose_line: You already finished Long Way Down. Now you are going back as an investigator.
instructions: Use each day's work to build your final board.
Begin by drawing, acting, mapping, speaking, or writing.

tasks.label: Project Question
tasks.prompt: What does Will decide at the end, and what evidence proves your thinking?

tasks.label: Entry Point
tasks.prompt: Choose: draw it, act it, map it, speak it, or write it. Explain why this helps you think.

tasks.label: Claim Builder
tasks.prompt: I think Will chooses to ______ because ______.

tasks.label: Completion Check
tasks.prompt: Check: claim, two ghosts, one symbol, one craft choice, theme, defence.

embedded_supports: This connects to the theme that...

graphic_organizer.title: Elevator Evidence Organizer
success_criteria: I connect evidence to my claim.

discussion_prep_sheet.title: Discussion Prep - What Does Will Decide?
discussion_prep_sheet.discussion_prompt: What does Will decide, and what evidence best supports your answer?

station_cards.title: Elevator Evidence Stations
instructions: Try more than one entry point.

cards.title: Map It
cards.task: Add one pressure word beside each ghost.

cards.title: Act It
cards.task: Show pressure, doubt, memory, or fear symbolically.

cards.title: Write It
cards.task: Circle the stronger claim and explain why.

success_criteria: I tried more than one way to think.

final_response_sheet.title: Final Defence - Elevator Evidence Board
final_response_sheet.prompt: Defend your answer using your board. You may write a paragraph or complete a teacher conference.
planning_reminders: Use two ghosts, one symbol, and one craft choice.

frame_strip: One ghost who affects him is...

rubric_sheet.title: Elevator Evidence Board Rubric
rubric_sheet.purpose_line: Assess the final project and defence.

criteria.name: Character impact
criteria.descriptor: Explains how two ghosts affect Will.

criteria.name: Symbolism
criteria.descriptor: Explains one symbol and connects it to meaning.

criteria.name: Craft choice
criteria.descriptor: Explains one author or artist choice.

sub_plan.title: Sub Notes - Elevator Evidence Project
sub_plan.class_context: Students are working on a post-reading evidence project for Long Way Down.
sub_plan.materials_location: Use the student packet and project folders.
sub_plan.classroom_notes: Keep work text-focused. Acting must be silent and symbolic.

steps.label: Settle
steps.what_to_say: Today you are improving one part of your evidence board.
steps.what_students_do: Open packet and write the date.

steps.label: Work block
steps.what_to_say: If stuck, choose one ghost or one symbol and explain what it shows.
steps.what_students_do: Work independently or with a partner.

sub_plan.if_stuck: Use: This shows Will might choose to ______ because ______.

answer_key.title: Teacher Keys - Elevator Evidence Project

entries.expected_answer: Buck, Dani, Uncle Mark, Will's father, Frick, and Shawn should be connected to pressure, memory, fear, consequence, cycle, or doubt.
acceptable_variants: Students may focus on four ghosts.
entries.teacher_note: Look for change in Will's thinking.

entries.expected_answer: Elevator can mean decision space; gun can mean inherited violence; smoke/shadow can mean grief or confusion; L button can mean exit or life choice.
No single correct answer. Students must support the claim with evidence.
acceptable_variants: Will stops, shoots, freezes, returns upstairs, or remains undecided.

scoring_guidance: Drawing, acting, mapping, and speaking must lead to explanation.

days.teacher_move: Students rebuild sequence using floor cards.
Students analyze two ghosts using entry choices.
Students sketch and explain one symbol.
Students explain one author or artist choice.
Students rehearse claims orally and physically.
